policies.rule: All brake repair jobs must be assigned to a mechanic with brake_service certification.
Electric bike repairs must be handled by a mechanic with electrical_service certification.
Brake and steering repairs should be marked as high priority.
Premium parts (over $25 per item) require manager approval before ordering.
Mechanics with a rating below 4.0 cannot be assigned to high-priority jobs.
Each mechanic can handle at most 3 active jobs at a time.
For brake pad replacement, always use the standard-grade pads unless the customer specifically requests premium.
Bicycles under warranty (purchased within 1 year) receive free labor on covered repairs.
Any bike with hydraulic brakes requiring bleeding must have the job flagged as high priority.
If a part is out of stock, it must be special-ordered before being added to a job.
Battery replacements for electric bikes require electrical_service certification and manager approval.
Brake rotor replacements must include new brake pads in the same job.
All repair jobs must be reviewed by the assigned mechanic before parts are ordered.
Total parts cost exceeding $100 requires customer approval before proceeding.
Same-day repairs are only available for high-priority jobs.
